description_long: This object is a compact, handheld two-way radio or walkie-talkie, featuring a rectangular black plastic body with rounded edges. The front face displays a small digital screen at the top, showing channel information and signal strength, and is accompanied by a grid of circular physical buttons below for input and control. An antenna extends vertically from the top right, while a smaller knob or button is positioned on the top left, likely for power or volume adjustment. The back side has a circular raised area, possibly a battery compartment or clip mount, and a rectangular panel near the bottom. The right side features a protruding button, likely for push-to-talk functionality, while the left side is smooth and unadorned. The overall design is utilitarian, with a matte finish and minimal decorative elements, suitable for rugged urban or outdoor use.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a digital display, physical buttons, and a prominent antenna, designed for portable communication.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, button grid, and antenna.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the protruding push-to-talk button and antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the circular battery compartment or clip mount and a rectangular panel.
description_view_3: Left side view showing a smooth, featureless surface and the antenna.
